page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-64GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance alongside weak single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places the instance in the bottom 10% with a score of 783.79, while multi-core performance reaches an average tier at 6240.61. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 833.5 GB/sec, but large-block uncached read bandwidth drops to a weak 32.5 GB/sec. Passmark memory testing also reflects this weakness, scoring in the bottom 25% at 1678.56.

This server is configured for workloads requiring significant memory capacity relative to compute power, such as database operations, caching layers, and text processing. Redis SET operations perform at an average rate of 11.88 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 6.07 GB/sec. However, the shared CPU allocation and poor single-core performance make it less suitable for compute-heavy tasks like medium-to-large language model inference, where text generation for a 7B model drops to a poor 5.2 tokens per second. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing KVM virtualization. It is configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6240.61, but weak single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources, such as database caching, web serving, and basic text processing. It is less suited for intensive compute tasks or large-scale language model inference due to CPU sharing and low single-core throughput.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-64GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-64GB is a KVM virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive, shared-CPU workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server with shared AMD EPYC processors, designed for memory-intensive workloads and database caching without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU resources
Average multi-core performance with weak single-core and uncached memory bandwidth
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
